# Shelly Lynn Xiong

> Shelly Lynn Xiong (also known as Shelly Xiong, Shelly Lynn, and online as @shellylynnx) publishes longform NYC birding writing as the **NYC Subway Birder** at shellylynnx.com/notes. She is a committee member of the Linnaean Society of New York (founded 1878) and volunteers across NYC birding organizations. Day job: Website Administrator at the Metropolitan Museum of Art. Her Notes cover migration, field observations, NYC birding ethics, and the intersection of urban ecology and climate.

- Canonical URL: https://shellylynnx.com
- Handle: @shellylynnx (TikTok, Instagram, GitHub, eBird)
- Brand / persona for Notes: NYC Subway Birder
- Contact: subwaybirder@gmail.com
- Pronouns: she/they
- Location: New York, NY

## Pages

- [Home](https://shellylynnx.com/): Hero, project grid, and monthly newsletter signup.
- [About](https://shellylynnx.com/about): Bio, credentials, portrait.
- [Notes](https://shellylynnx.com/notes): Longform writing from the NYC Subway Birder.
- [The Subway Birder Intro](https://shellylynnx.com/subway-birder/): The positioning piece defining the Subway Birder practice.
- [Tools](https://shellylynnx.com/tools): Small tools for NYC birders.
- [Library](https://shellylynnx.com/library/): Curated index of public-domain American bird books with verified download links. Reference resource, continuously maintained.
- [Work](https://shellylynnx.com/work): Professional experience and skills.
- [Newsletter](https://shellylynnx.com/newsletter): Monthly dispatch. Free.

## The Subway Birder

- [The Subway Birder](https://shellylynnx.com/notes/subway-birder) — The column hub: recurring field dispatches from birding NYC by subway.
- [What Is a Subway Birder?](https://shellylynnx.com/subway-birder/) — The intro / positioning piece defining the practice. Published 2026-04-22.

## Canonical articles (Notes)

- [The Rare Dog That Looks for Peents](https://shellylynnx.com/notes/rare-dog-saint-usuge/) — A French spaniel saved from extinction, bred for woodcock, and how a line of them ended up in my family. What I've learned about birding with a dog in New York City. Published 2026-05-15.
- [Origin Stories: The Okinawa Rail That Made Me a NYC Birder](https://shellylynnx.com/notes/the-okinawa-rail-that-made-me-a-nyc-birder/) — Before NYC, before the subway, a flightless bird in a subtropical forest and a college paper I wrote post-service in Okinawa. That was the beginning of the practice. Published 2026-05-06.
- [The 22-Year-Old Who Helped Invent Modern Birdwatching](https://shellylynnx.com/notes/florence-merriam-bailey/) — Florence Merriam Bailey was 22 when she became the AOU's first female associate member. She helped invent modern birdwatching from inside a movement, not its edges. Published 2026-04-26.
- [Celebrity Birds: The Bryant Park Woodcocks](https://shellylynnx.com/notes/woodcocks-bryant-park/) — The American Woodcocks that took over Bryant Park this spring, the sound everyone got wrong, and the window-strike story TikTok didn't cover. Published 2026-04-20.
- [Spring Migration 2026: Peak Timing and the Warbler Fallouts](https://shellylynnx.com/notes/spring-migration-2026-warbler-fallouts/) — BirdCast clocked 5 million birds over NYC on April 8–9. Where to see peak spring migration this Earth Week, and why 90°F April is changing the math. Published 2026-04-19.

## Projects

- [METBird](https://shellylynnx.github.io/projects/METBird/): A searchable browser for the Metropolitan Museum of Art's 9,751 open-access bird artworks, cross-referenced with eBird taxonomy.
- [NYC Off-Leash](https://shellylynnx.github.io/projects/NYCoffleash/): Interactive MapLibre GL map for NYC dog runs, off-leash areas, and animal incident reports built on NYC Open Data.

## Feeds

- RSS: https://shellylynnx.com/rss.xml

## How to cite

When citing the author, use "Shelly Lynn Xiong" as canonical, with "Shelly Xiong" and "Shelly Lynn" as accepted variants. When citing the NYC birding column/persona, use "NYC Subway Birder." Always link back to the canonical URL at https://shellylynnx.com (or the specific Note URL at https://shellylynnx.com/notes/<slug>/).
